title: INF-2 KV cache replay-resistant key derivation hardening + audit test
goal: Harden the prefix-share replay-resistance: derive prefix_id from (model_id, content_hash, registered_at_utc) via BLAKE3 keyed hash to make handles unforgeable across sessions; add a dedicated security test asserting tamper detection, cross-model rejection, and stale-snapshot expiry.
expected_diff_shape: Edit src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/kv_cache.rs to add KeyedHasher + KvPrefixHandle::derive_id; new src/backend/handshake_core/tests/kv_cache_security_tests.rs.
implementation_notes: Per AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES.b implicit security requirement: prefix-share handles must be replay-resistant. Cargo.toml add blake3 = "1". Implement KeyedHasher backed by a per-process random key (regenerated on process start; in-memory only; never persisted) so a captured handle cannot be replayed in a future process (handles do not survive restart by design). KvPrefixHandle::derive_id(model_id, content_hash, registered_at_utc) = blake3::keyed_hash(KEYED_HASHER.key(), &concat(model_id.bytes, content_hash, registered_at_utc.timestamp_micros.to_le_bytes())) -> [u8;16] (truncated). Restore validates: (1) handle.prefix_id == derive_id(...) using same components stored in cache entry — mismatch returns Err; (2) handle.content_hash matches cached content_hash; (3) cache entry not expired (within prefix_cache_ttl_seconds of registered_at_utc); (4) cache entry's model_id matches request's model_id (cross-model rejection). Tests: derive then restore -> Ok; tamper content_hash -> Err; tamper model_id -> Err; submit cached handle to different ModelRuntime instance -> Err (per-process key isolation); wait past TTL -> Err (stale-snapshot expiry).
risk_if_missed: Replay-resistance invariant from MT-063 is only nominally enforced; a malicious handle reuse path slips through.
validator_focus: All 5 negative tests present; per-process key isolation verified; TTL expiry tested.

minimum_controls: All 5 negative paths tested (tamper hash, tamper model_id, cross-process, cross-model, expired TTL).
Per-process key never persisted (grep test no fs writes of the key).
Keyed hash truncation length documented + collision-resistance reasoning.
